mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in industrial production reflects broad economic expansion and improving corporate earnings outlooks, leading to positive equity returns over the medium term as this strength is priced in.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO is a core component of the Coincident Economic Activity Index.
failure_condition: The rank information coefficient between INDPRO 3-month change and 60-day forward returns remains below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals a sharp economic contraction and deteriorating consumer demand, leading to negative equity returns as earnings expectations are revised down.
evidence_refs: fact:The Sahm Rule uses rapid UNRATE increases to identify recessions in real-time.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances following a trigger event over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation accelerates while the central bank fails to tighten policy, real rates fall and inflation expectations can become unanchored. This stagflationary environment is negative for equities due to margin compression and policy uncertainty.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY % change > 4% AND FEDFUNDS 3-month change <= 0
failure_condition: The mechanism fails if triggered periods do not show statistically significant underperformance versus non-triggered periods (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In an early-cycle recovery, accelerating retail sales signal a robust consumer and ample room for economic growth before inflationary pressures build, leading to strong positive equity returns.
failure_condition: The signal's positive correlation with forward returns disappears or reverses when the unemployment rate is below 5%.

mechanism_narrative: Strong nonfarm payrolls growth indicates a robust labor market, which fuels consumer spending and corporate revenue growth, translating into higher equity prices over the next month.
evidence_refs: fact:The monthly jobs report is a key market-moving data release.
failure_condition: A 3-year rolling correlation between PAYEMS 3-month growth and S&P 500 20-day forward returns is consistently negative or statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: A significant year-over-year decline in housing starts signals falling demand in a key interest-rate sensitive sector, often preceding a wider economic slowdown and negatively impacting investor sentiment.
evidence_refs: fact:Housing sector weakness preceded the 2008 recession.
failure_condition: Triggered periods do not lead to negative average 60-day forward returns over a full business cycle.

mechanism_narrative: An upside surprise in the latest quarterly real GDP growth figure confirms strong aggregate demand, supporting positive equity market momentum over a very short horizon as investors extrapolate recent strength.
failure_condition: The average 5-day forward return following a trigger is not statistically different from the unconditional average return (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the labor market is at full employment (low unemployment), rising inflation forces aggressive monetary tightening to prevent wage-price spirals. This policy response is highly detrimental to equity valuations.
failure_condition: The signal fails if triggered periods are not followed by a negative average 60-day return and an increase in the FEDFUNDS rate within the subsequent 6 months.
